title: Lasiagrostis
taxonomy: Lasiagrostis is a genus of grasses, belonging to the family Poaceae. This genus is part of the subfamily Pooideae. Lasiagrostis is commonly found in temperate to alpine regions around the world. The classification of Lasiagrostis is further divided into several species, each with unique characteristics. The classification of Lasiagrostis is primarily based on its morphology and geographic distribution.
uses: Lasiagrostis, a genus of grasses, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In some cultures, Lasiagrostis is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits. However, I found information about Lasiagrostis's close relative, Lasiagrostis (also spelled as Lasiagrostis or Lasia) is actually a different name for the genus of grasses known as Stipagrostis and/or Lasiagrostis is sometimes used as a synonym for the genus Stipagrostis.
